skill.name: kubernetes-deployment
skill.description: Guides Kubernetes deployment planning across containers, manifests, Helm charts, service mesh, security, observability, and GitOps rollout steps.
skill.summary: Plan production Kubernetes deployments with structured phases and quality gates.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, a K8s description string, and a checklist item as risky patterns. Manual review found no executable code, network access, filesystem mutation, secret handling, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick References
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Markdown code spans, skill names, and fenced prompt examples. They are not Ruby backtick execution and do not execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain Markdown formatting and plain prompt text only. No executable script, interpreter directive, or command invocation syntax is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert points to the front matter description, which mentions K8s configurations. No cryptographic algorithm or implementation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is descriptive metadata about Kubernetes deployment. It contains no cryptographic operation, algorithm selection, or security-sensitive code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Match
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert points to a checklist item for validating Kubernetes manifests. It does not instruct the model to inspect host identity, users, processes, or network state.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a deployment quality gate, not a host reconnaissance command. The surrounding checklist covers normal Kubernetes deployment readiness.

content.value_statement: Kubernetes releases often fail because container, manifest, security, and rollout work happens in separate steps. This skill provides a phased deployment workflow with prompts and quality gates for production planning.
actual_capabilities: Organizes Kubernetes deployment work into container, manifest, Helm, service mesh, security, observability, and rollout phases.
Lists related specialist skills to invoke for each deployment phase.
Provides copy-paste prompts for containerization, manifest generation, Helm scaffolding, Istio, security, monitoring, and GitOps tasks.
Defines quality gates for containers, manifests, Helm installation, security, monitoring, and deployment success.
Connects Kubernetes deployment work to adjacent cloud, Terraform, and Docker workflows.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts, generated manifests, or Helm chart templates.
Depends on separate referenced skills for detailed implementation work.
Does not validate a live cluster or inspect Kubernetes resources directly.
Does not choose provider-specific settings for managed Kubernetes services.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Kubernetes Release
use_cases.description: Create a deployment sequence that covers images, manifests, Helm packaging, security, monitoring, and rollout checks.

use_cases.title: Standardize Platform Delivery
use_cases.description: Use one deployment checklist across teams so Kubernetes releases follow the same readiness gates.

use_cases.title: Prepare Production Migration
use_cases.description: Map existing container workloads into Kubernetes phases before moving them into a cluster.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Deployment Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this Kubernetes deployment workflow to plan a release for my application. Include phases, owners, and quality gates.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a first Kubernetes deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Phase Prompts
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this workflow into task prompts for container preparation, manifests, Helm chart creation, security, monitoring, and GitOps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Coordinating work across multiple specialist skills.

prompt_templates.title: Review Deployment Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the workflow quality gates to review my planned Kubernetes deployment. Identify missing steps and release risks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Production Rollout Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Adapt this Kubernetes deployment workflow for a production service with Helm, service mesh, mTLS, Prometheus, Grafana, and GitOps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced deployment planning for a mature platform.

output_examples.input: Plan a Kubernetes deployment for a web API.
output_examples.output: A phased release plan covering image preparation, manifests, Helm packaging, ingress, RBAC, monitoring, GitOps rollout, and final readiness checks.

output: Missing network policy and RBAC review.
Helm installation test should happen before production rollout.
Monitoring and alert checks should be completed before traffic shift.

output_examples.output: A workflow that selects the mesh, enables traffic management, configures mTLS, adds observability, and verifies deployment health.

best_practices: Start with container health and image size before writing deployment manifests.
Validate security, observability, and rollout gates before sending production traffic.
Use GitOps only after manifests and Helm charts have been reviewed.
anti_patterns: Skipping security policy work until after the first production release.
Deploying Helm charts without a separate installation test.
Treating monitoring as optional instead of a release requirement.

faq.question: Does this skill deploy to a Kubernetes cluster directly?
faq.answer: No. It is a workflow guide and prompt set. It does not run kubectl, Helm, or cluster commands.

faq.question: Can it generate Kubernetes manifests?
faq.answer: It points users to a manifest generation phase and related specialist skill. It does not include manifest templates itself.

faq.question: Is Helm covered?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow includes chart structure, values, templates, dependencies, and installation testing.

faq.question: Does it support service mesh planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes phases for mesh selection, traffic management, mTLS, and observability.

faq.question: What security topics are included?
faq.answer: The workflow covers RBAC, NetworkPolicy, PodSecurity, secrets, and mTLS planning.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: It is useful for application engineers, platform engineers, and DevOps teams planning Kubernetes releases.
